Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals heavy proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting nephrotic syndrome or severe diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Blood tests show metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride). Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.
